remember: Students will be able to define international trade and its importance.
Students will be able to recall the advantages and disadvantages of international trade.
Students will be able to identify the major trading blocs and countries.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of balance of trade and balance of payments.
Students will be able to describe the role of World Trade Organization (WTO) in international trade.
Students will be able to understand the impact of international trade on a country's economy.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concepts of international trade to real-life scenarios.
Students will be able to analyze the trade policies of different countries and their impact on international trade.
Students will be able to evaluate the benefits and drawbacks of trade agreements and organizations.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the factors that influence international trade, such as tariffs, quotas, and exchange rates.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the trade patterns of different countries and regions.
Students will be able to assess the impact of international trade on the environment and society.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the concept and importance of international trade.
To learn about the organization and management of international trade, including the role of WTO.
To analyze the impact of international trade on the economy and environment of a country.
board_exam_objectives: CBSE tests the ability to define and explain key terms related to international trade.
CBSE evaluates the understanding of the concepts of balance of trade, balance of payments, and trade policies.
CBSE assesses the ability to analyze and apply the concepts of international trade to real-life scenarios and case studies.
